boundary_route_fanin.read: Actual LightningIndexer top-k fan-in is counted per compressed group from the TorchTitan-shaped boundary tensors. Prefix stats are the structured route used by earlier synthetic dKV probes at the same top-k; matching totals do not imply matching topology.

decision_read: This is a route-only upper-bound estimate for reducing dKV atomics inside a query-major tile. It counts how many valid (query token, selected compressed group) pairs collapse to unique compressed groups inside each tile. It does not include the extra register/shared-memory pressure needed to hold D-dimensional partial accumulators, so weak reduction factors are strong negative evidence, while strong factors still need a kernel probe. The hot_group_hybrid_reduction fields model a bounded local table: only the N most frequent groups in each tile are reduced locally, while all tail route pairs keep the current direct atomic path.

per_token_uniqueness.read: Per-token duplicate groups are expected to be near zero because LightningIndexer top-k returns unique indices. Any useful coalescing therefore has to come from grouping multiple query tokens in the same kernel tile.
